compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Plan-review PASSED at HEAD dec75f22 (run 4; epic 3156 also re-attested + now in_progress). Advisory triage: the 15 residual advisories are plan-text-vs-implementation deltas — the description still describes the earlier module-dict/mtime memo + the abandoned batch.extra_criteria fan-out, but the landed implementation already adopts the BETTER form each advisory asks for: (1) effective_routing/load_criteria are @lru_cache(maxsize=128) keyed by (repo_root, sha256(overlay-bytes)) — content-hash not mtime, bounded eviction not an unbounded dict; (2) prompt_library._invalidate_caches() clears both new lru_caches (test_editing_overlay_invalidates + test asserts); (3) project fan-out is runner-side (ProductionBatchRunner appends the project. subset of route_criteria) since the v3 batch schema is immutable + the gate self-validates — gates/plan-review.yaml + the schema are UNCHANGED; include_project_<name> is the sanitized coverage/routing record. Re-editing the description to chase each advisory would re-invalidate the just-earned attestation (material change) and loop, so captured here instead. registry_version(repo_root) overlay-hashing + attestation invalidation is explicitly story 08af (not this MVP root).

compiled_state.description:
## Why

Today a plan-review criterion is inert unless it is in the hardcoded `CANONICAL_LLM` frozenset
(`plan_review/registry.py:70`) AND the packaged `criteria_routing.json`; `_routing_index()` /
`load_criteria()` are `@lru_cache`d with no `repo_root` key. So a project cannot add a criterion at
all. This story opens the vocabulary and adds the project overlay — the **dependency root + MVP
slice** for the whole epic (`3156`). It is NOT blocked on `b744` (extends plan-review directly).

## What

- `effective_criteria(repo_root)` = `CANONICAL_LLM` ∪ activated project ids; route it through
  **every** plan-review vocabulary site: `load_criteria`, `check_registry_coverage`, and the
  workflow Pass-1 batch vocab in `workflow_ops` (`plan_review_assemble_criteria`). The
  `include_<id>` output key must sanitize the `project.<name>` id (`.`→`_`) at BOTH the assemble
  and consume sites (keep the rule co-located).
- `.rebar/criteria_routing.json` overlay: gate-keyed (`{plan_review: {...}, activate: [...]}`),
  reusing the packaged routing schema; `project.<name>` namespace; an un-prefixed built-in id =
  re-tune/disable; a `project.`-id colliding with a built-in, or a duplicate, is **rejected at
  load** (located error). A criterion runs only if in `activate`.
- Cache isolation: keep `_routing_index()` cached for the packaged index; add
  `effective_routing(repo_root)` (uncached, or keyed by `(repo_root, overlay mtime)` — note mtime
  granularity, prefer content hash if flaky); extend `prompt_library._invalidate_caches()`.
- Routing validation: load-time (invalid routing = located error, not silent skip) + a CI
  drift/parity gate mirroring `reviewers/index.json`.

## Scope

- Files: `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py`, `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/workflow_ops.py`,
  `src/rebar/llm/prompt_library.py`, `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json` (schema doc).
- MVP end-to-end: activate ONE project LLM criterion from the overlay → it runs in plan-review
  Pass-1 → surfaces a finding. (Attestation invalidation is story `#4`; unify is story `#6`.)
- Out: code-review side (story `#6`, b744-blocked); DET consumer (story `#2`).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `effective_criteria(repo_root)` loads activated project criteria and is routed through
      `load_criteria`, `check_registry_coverage`, and the workflow Pass-1 batch vocab; an activated
      project LLM criterion fans out and runs in plan-review Pass-1.
- [ ] `.rebar/criteria_routing.json` overlay per schema: gate-keyed, `project.<name>` namespace,
      explicit `activate` list, load-time collision/rebind rejection (located error).
- [ ] `include_<id>` key sanitization (`.`→`_`) applied at both assemble + consume sites.
- [ ] Overlay merge is cache-isolated per repo (packaged index stays cached; overlay is not);
      a RED test proves no cross-repo routing leakage in the long-lived MCP server.
- [ ] Routing validation: invalid overlay routing is a located error + a CI drift/parity gate.
- [ ] An ADR documents the unified-registry + overlay merge/activation contract.
- [ ] MVP slice demonstrated end-to-end (activate one criterion → runs → surfaces a finding).

## Backward-compatibility, fan-out mechanism & cache strategy (remediation, run 1)

### Expand-contract / backward-compatibility (clears T4)
Every signature change is **additive** with a `repo_root=None` default that preserves
today's behaviour, so no existing caller breaks:
- `registry.load_criteria(repo_root=None)`, `registry.by_id(repo_root=None)`,
  `registry.check_registry_coverage(repo_root=None)`, `registry._descriptor_from_prompt(cid,
  *, repo_root=None, routing_index=None)`, and the NEW `effective_criteria(repo_root=None)` /
  `effective_routing(repo_root=None)` all default `repo_root=None`.
- `repo_root=None` resolves to `config.repo_root()` (the same root `get_prompt` already
  resolves `.rebar/prompts/` overrides against) — so an **overlay-absent** repo yields
  **exactly** today's packaged-only vocabulary/routing (no behaviour change, no churn).
- **Call sites updated** (enumerated): `orchestrator.route_criteria` (passes the ctx
  repo_root), `workflow_ops.plan_review_assemble_criteria` (uses `ctx.repo_root`),
  `attest.registry_version`/`plan_review.__init__` coverage (story #4 threads repo_root;
  this story keeps the no-arg default working), and the tests. Bare `load_criteria()` /
  `check_registry_coverage()` keep working unchanged.
- **Rollback:** delete `.rebar/criteria_routing.json` (overlay-absent ⇒ packaged behaviour),
  or revert the additive params (all default `None`).

### Dynamic Pass-1 fan-out mechanism (clears G6/T1/T3 — schema-safe, NO v3 change)
The v3 `batch` step schema (`workflow.v3.schema.json`) is IMMUTABLE + version-pinned with
`additionalProperties:false` on `$defs/batch`, and the gate validates its OWN document at run
time — so a new `batch` field (e.g. `extra_criteria`) is impossible without a v4 schema+shim
(out of MVP scope). Instead, project criteria fan out through the **rebar-specific
`ProductionBatchRunner`** (`production_batch_runner.py`), NOT a new doc field:
- built-in criteria keep fanning out via the STATIC `criteria:` list in `gates/plan-review.yaml`
  (each gated by its `include_<ID>` key — byte-identical to today);
- the runner, after `assemble_context`, calls `route_criteria(ctx)` (which now returns the FULL
  effective set) and appends ONLY the `project.<name>`-prefixed descriptors — already past the
  SAME `applies()`/overlay filter the built-ins use — to the finder's tier-split set, deduped
  against the built-ins. `assemble` still emits sanitized `include_project_<name>` booleans
  (`.`→`_`) as the coverage/routing record. `gates/plan-review.yaml` and the immutable schema
  are UNTOUCHED. (Probe-mode caveat: route_criteria has no probe notion, so a project criterion
  is also evaluated during a drift probe — harmless, documented follow-up.)

### Cache strategy — content-hash, not mtime (clears E2/G6/T8/T9)
`effective_routing`/`load_criteria` are memoized in repo-keyed module dicts keyed by
`(repo_root, sha256(overlay-bytes))` — a **content signature**, NOT mtime (mtime
granularity is coarse/flaky), so editing the overlay auto-invalidates the memo. The
packaged `_routing_index()` stays `@lru_cache`d (immutable per binary). Stale-entry
growth is bounded: `prompt_library._invalidate_caches()` clears both memos + the lru_caches
(the RED cross-repo-leak test proves repo A's routing never serves repo B).

### Sanitization inverse & missing-prompt fallback (clears T8)
The `.`→`_` map is one-directional at the workflow-key layer only; the criterion id and
prompt id retain the dotted form, so no inverse un-sanitization is needed (the
`project_criteria` entry carries the real dotted `prompt` id directly). An activated
project id whose prompt file is absent/malformed surfaces as a **located `RegistryError`**
from `load_criteria` (same as a missing built-in prompt) — fail-loud at load, never a
silent skip.

### ADR contents (clears E1/AC6)
`docs/adr/0015-project-supplied-criteria.md` documents: the `.rebar/criteria_routing.json`
overlay schema (gate-keyed, `project.<name>` namespace, `activate` list); the merge +
collision/rebind rules; `effective_criteria`/`effective_routing` as the vocabulary seam;
the content-hash cache-isolation contract; and the runner-side (schema-free) project fan-out.

### Scope clarification (clears E5/T8/G5)
Files also touched: `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/production_batch_runner.py` (project fan-in) and `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py` (`route_criteria` threads repo_root). MVP end-to-end exercises ONE activated project **LLM** criterion (`exec: 1-TURN`). Project
`exec: DET` criteria (the prompt-less descriptor branch) are story #2; the shared
`rebar.llm.criteria` unification is story #6. The CI drift/parity gate here is a
**validation** gate (the packaged `criteria_routing.json` stays schema-valid and in parity
with `CANONICAL_LLM` + the prompt files), not a generator, since the routing is
hand-authored judgement (thresholds/applies_at) with no derivation source.

## Acceptance Criteria (remediation additions)

- [ ] All new/changed registry signatures are additive with a `repo_root=None` default that
      preserves packaged-only behaviour (overlay-absent repo == today); rollback = delete the
      overlay.
- [ ] The dynamic Pass-1 fan-out for a project criterion is runner-side (`ProductionBatchRunner`
      appends the `project.` subset of `route_criteria`), leaving the immutable v3 `batch` schema
      and `gates/plan-review.yaml` UNCHANGED.

- [ ] An activated project criterion with a missing/malformed prompt file raises a LOCATED
      `RegistryError` from `load_criteria` (fail-loud, no silent skip); a test asserts it.
- [ ] Overlay mis-authoring is covered by named tests: collision/rebind rejection, un-namespaced
      net-new id, activated-without-routing, invalid routing schema, malformed JSON — each a
      located `RegistryError`.
- [ ] `prompt_library._invalidate_caches()` clears the new overlay-merged caches (proven: an
      overlay edit is picked up for the same repo_root without a process restart).

file_impact.reason: effective_criteria/effective_routing + overlay load/validate + cache isolation + parity gate

compiled_state.title: Criteria registry: open the vocabulary + .rebar/ routing overlay + activation + cache isolation (plan-review MVP root)

verify_commands.dd_text: packaged routing in parity with CANONICAL_LLM
